nodes.context: Predominantly Mexican American prison gang and criminal organization in the United States, based in California prison system.
Notoriously brutal Hispanic street gang in Southern California, under influence of Mexican Mafia.
Hispanic street gang in Southern California, under influence of Mexican Mafia.
Prison gang founded by Mexican-American inmates in the mid-1960s at California Correctional Training Facility in Soledad, rival of Mexican Mafia.
Arizona prison gang formed around 1974, split from Original Mexican Mafia in 1978.
One of two factions after 1978 split of Mexican Mafia in Arizona, kept original philosophy.
White prison gang, loose ally of Mexican Mafia due to common rivals.
Prison gang, rival of Mexican Mafia, loose ally of Nuestra Familia.
Street gang, new enemy of Mexican Mafia.
Street gang, new enemy of Mexican Mafia.
Vassal gangs of Mexican Mafia in Southern California.
Hispanic street gang in East Los Angeles, associated with Mexican Mafia.
Hispanic street gang in Highland Park, Los Angeles, carried out murders on orders of Mexican Mafia.
Italian-American organized crime family in Los Angeles, collaborated with Mexican Mafia.
Criminal organization, ally of Mexican Mafia per infobox.
Italian-American crime family, ally of Mexican Mafia per infobox.
Outlaw motorcycle club, ally (1960s–1980s) and later rival (1980s–present) of Mexican Mafia per infobox.
Criminal organization, ally of Mexican Mafia per infobox.
Prison gang, ally of Mexican Mafia per infobox.
Outlaw motorcycle club, ally of Mexican Mafia per infobox.
Mexican drug cartel, ally of Mexican Mafia per infobox.
Mexican drug cartel, ally of Mexican Mafia per infobox.
Rival gang of Mexican Mafia per infobox.
Mexican drug cartel, rival of Mexican Mafia per infobox.
Predominantly Mexican American prison gang and criminal organization in the United States, deadliest and most powerful gang within the California prison system.
Hispanic street gang in Southern California, notoriously brutal.
Hispanic street gang in Southern California.
Gang associated with the Mexican Mafia; member Benjamin 'Topo' Peters was a high-ranking eMe member.
Gang whose members were victims of a shooting by Mexican Mafia member Alfred Sandoval.
Gang from Ontario, California; member Ruben 'Tupi' Hernandez was a Mexican Mafia member.
Gang from Escondido, California; member Rudy 'Crazy' Espudo was a Mexican Mafia boss.
Gang associated with the Mexican Mafia, indicted in 2011 for harassing and intimidating black people.
Gang in the Los Angeles Harbor Region; investigation led to arrests of Mexican Mafia members.
Prison gang inspired by real-life counterpart in the film Blood In Blood Out.
Prison gang inspired by real-life counterpart in the film Blood In Blood Out.

edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican Mafia has immense influence and control over every Hispanic street gang in Southern California, including the notoriously brutal MS-13 and 18th Street Gang
edges.context: Mexican Mafia has influence and control over MS-13.

edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican Mafia has immense influence and control over every Hispanic street gang in Southern California, including the notoriously brutal MS-13 and 18th Street Gang
edges.context: Mexican Mafia has influence and control over 18th Street Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: The primary rivals of the Mexican Mafia are Nuestra Familia.
edges.context: Mexican Mafia and Nuestra Familia are primary rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican Mafia also holds a loose alliance with the Aryan Brotherhood, mainly due to their common rivals within the prison system.
edges.context: Mexican Mafia has a loose alliance with Aryan Brotherhood due to common rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican Mafia is also a rival of the Black Guerrilla Family prison gang
edges.context: Mexican Mafia is a rival of Black Guerrilla Family.

edges.evidence_quote: Bloods and Crips are the new enemies.
edges.context: Bloods are new enemies of Mexican Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Bloods and Crips are the new enemies.
edges.context: Crips are new enemies of Mexican Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican Mafia is the controlling organization for almost every Hispanic gang in Southern California, ... with their vassal gangs being called Sureños.
edges.context: Mexican Mafia controls Sureños as vassal gangs.

edges.evidence_quote: Many of the street level homicides in the Highland Park area of Los Angeles committed by the Avenues gang were done on orders issued by the Mexican Mafia.
edges.context: Mexican Mafia orders murders carried out by The Avenues.

edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican Mafia and the Italian-American Los Angeles crime family collaborated in skimming money from Get Going, a taxpayer-funded drug treatment program.
edges.context: Mexican Mafia collaborated with Los Angeles crime family in skimming money.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1978 the Mexican Mafia split into two organizations. One kept the original philosophy and structure and currently refer to themselves as the Original Mexican Mafia, "Califas Faction", "EME".
edges.context: Original Mexican Mafia is a splinter faction of the Mexican Mafia in Arizona.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1978 the Mexican Mafia split into two organizations. ... The other, which came into prominence in 1984 and refer to themselves as the New Mexican Mafia.
edges.context: New Mexican Mafia is a splinter faction of the Mexican Mafia in Arizona.

edges.evidence_quote: On January 31, 2022, a fight broke out between members of the Mexican Mafia and MS-13 at United States Penitentiary, Beaumont, Texas, causing two fatalities
edges.context: Mexican Mafia and MS-13 fought at a prison, causing two fatalities.

edges.evidence_quote: Alfred Arthur 'Chato' Sandoval, a high ranking eMe member and Arizona Maravilla gangster
edges.context: Arizona Maravilla is a gang whose member is also a high-ranking Mexican Mafia member.

edges.evidence_quote: The victims were members of the Mariana Marivilla gang.
edges.context: Mexican Mafia member killed members of Mariana Marivilla.

edges.evidence_quote: Benjamin 'Topo' Peters (Arizona Maravilla), was allegedly the Mexican Mafia's highest-ranking member at the time, and was engaged in a power struggle with fellow member Ruben 'Tupi' Hernandez (Onterio Varrio Sur – Ontario, California).
edges.context: Mexican Mafia member Ruben Hernandez is from Onterio Varrio Sur.

edges.evidence_quote: Rudy 'Crazy' Espudo (Esco Varrio Diablos – Escondido, California) was in control of the Hispanic gangs in the area and forced drug dealers to pay taxes in tribute to La eMe
edges.context: Esco Varrio Diablos member Rudy Espudo was a Mexican Mafia boss controlling local gangs.

edges.evidence_quote: Members of the Azusa 13 gang, associated with the Mexican Mafia, were indicted in 2011 for harassing and intimidating black people in Southern California.
edges.context: Azusa 13 is associated with the Mexican Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: A joint investigation by the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) and the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) into the Westside Wilmas gang in the Los Angeles Harbor Region commenced in October 2022 and culminated with the arrests of 12 Mexican Mafia members
edges.context: Investigation into Westside Wilmas led to arrests of Mexican Mafia members.
